items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: CLSA on Kotak Bk O-P, TP Rs 440 Announced acquisition of Deutsche Bank (DB) India’s retail banking, private banking and wealth manage...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
CLSA on Kotak Bk
O-P, TP Rs 440
Announced acquisition of Deutsche Bank (DB) India’s retail banking, private banking and wealth management business
This business comprised Rs290bn in loans, Rs160bn in deposits and Rs105bn in wealth AUM as of FY26
Purchase price for this business was Rs2.8bn plus the difference in assets and liabilities acquired as of the purchase date.
However, no detail about the profitability of the business was provided by co
Only comment made was it would be “ROE accretive”.

HSBC on Kotak BK
Buy, TP Rs 460
KMB announced acquisition of Deutsche Bank’s India retail banking portfolio for a consideration of INR2.8bn
Portfolio adds c5.6% of loans, c2.8% of deposits, & c1.7% CASA to KMB’s existing balance sheet as of FY26
This acquisition does not meaningfully change outlook on stock

Nomura on Kotak BK
Buy, TP Rs 460
Kotak to acquire DBAG's retail, private banking & wealth business in India
Disciplined pricing: close to book value pricing reflects DBAG’s soft profitability profile
Good secured asset book, but a comparatively weaker liability franchise
Capital impact manageable; management guides to RoE accretion
View this is a sensible, disciplined acquisition, but don't see RoE/earnings benefit as substantial or immediate; success hinges on integration execution & client retention

Jefferies on Kotak Bk
Buy, TP Rs 450
Kotak Bank will acquire Deutsche Bank's India retail bkg, private bkg, & WM business for Rs2.8bn.
To Kotak's loans/deposits/AUM, it adds 6%/3%/1%; takes 84bps of CET1.
Deal looks accretive with scope to cross-sell; it comes days after Kotak's CEO chose not to renew his term after Dec-26
Conversation with Jefferies European Bk analyst indicates this trx may be aimed to simplify DB's structure & focus on core markets
@CNBCTV18Live @_RituSingh

items.title: The Chairman provides oversight. The CEO provides execution. The moment a Chairman starts running the bank, or a CEO starts dominating the board, you ...
items.description_html:
The Chairman provides oversight. The CEO provides execution.

The moment a Chairman starts running the bank, or a CEO starts dominating the board, you have a governance problem.

Checks and balances work only when both understand the very different parts they have to play.


R. Balakrishnan: Why do banks need to appoint a Chairman ? The CEO is where the buck stops. once a Chairman (part time or full time) collects wages, he becomes the defacto CEO and should be held accountable for all the good and the bad. What will he do sitting in the office?

items.title: Coforge , some interesting highlights from the management in recent conference call that tell us a lot about the company as well and the sector too 1)...
items.description_html:
Coforge , some interesting highlights from the management in recent conference call that tell us a lot about the company as well and the sector too

1) They have defined and believe that 6 MOAT's will help the company actually benefit from the massive AI opportunity and have spelled them out really well ( do give it a read to also understand where the sector is tilting not only coforge )

2) Focusing on delivering 100% FCF/PAT conversion in FY27 , with good profitability growth H2 Heavy

3) They are expecting a flattish Q1 for numbers , there is a low-margin portfolio in India that we will discontinue immediately, and the negative impact of that will flow into Q1. Despite that significant portfolio, we expect to be flattish in Q1 and to be on a very fast growth trajectory from Q2 onwards ( again pointing to H2 heavy growth )

But what is interesting is downscaling low margin business is also brave in a sector that is not finding incremental deal wins , focusing on Encora Integration , pivoting to higher quality businesses are all steps in the right direction for terminal value

Also if you haven't already, do check out their investor day decks that they have detailed and key insights about their strategy going forward , see link below

https://investors.coforge.com/investor-day-2026

disc: invested and biased , no reco , no financial advice in any form

items.title: AI in my opinion is creating a lot of illusion of research that seems very good , many had to read years of annual reports , transcripts , visit on gr...
items.description_html:
AI in my opinion is creating a lot of illusion of research that seems very good , many had to read years of annual reports , transcripts , visit on ground and do a lot more to get a grasp of the company , while there is abundance of Data there is lacking insight and ability to have a stand and opinion , when data is so abundant , surface level data collection is called research by many

In fact this will make the people who think and have a strong process and edge only sharper , because reports can be made , illusion of research can be achieved but you cannot get conviction and courage using a prompt , those two variables will still be independent , so focus on the edge , use AI to help not to think !

items.title: Wrongful Removal of YouTube Channel @YouTubeIndia @YouTubeCreators @YTCreatorsIndia @TeamYouTube I am writing to formally appeal the recent notice sta...
items.description_html:
Wrongful Removal of YouTube Channel

@YouTubeIndia @YouTubeCreators @YTCreatorsIndia @TeamYouTube

I am writing to formally appeal the recent notice stating that my channel, Beat The Street, is under review for a violation of the "Harmful or Dangerous" content policy.

Currently, I have not received a notification pointing to any specific video, description, or timestamp that triggered this flag. Because of this, I am reaching out to request a manual human review of my channel to clear up what I believe is an automated misunderstanding.

Beat The Street is an educational channel dedicated to financial analysis, equity research, and corporate case studies. The content is strictly designed to educate viewers on capital markets and business strategies.

We absolutely do not depict, promote, or encourage any activities causing physical injury, death, or psychological harm. It is highly probable that YouTube’s automated system has misinterpreted standard financial terminology or metaphors used in our video titles and descriptions (such as "market bleeding," "corporate suicide," or "destroying the competition") and flagged them out of context.

I am fully committed to maintaining a safe community on YouTube. If a human reviewer determines that a specific piece of metadata or content is problematic, I am completely willing to edit or remove it immediately.

Could you please look into this case or clarify which specific content triggered the review so I can rectify it?

Thank you for your time, support, and guidance.

Sincerely,
Sudarshan Bhandari
Email ID: beetingdastreet@gmail.com

Channel Name: Beat The Street
Channel Link: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCpCE9fZxdQy92Z5AWJ3zSaQ

items.title: KPIT is down 16% today, after a weak H1FY27 outlook Stock is now available at a Market Cap of around 15k Cr At one point, KPIT commanded a Market Cap ...
items.description_html:
KPIT is down 16% today, after a weak H1FY27 outlook

Stock is now available at a Market Cap of around 15k Cr

At one point, KPIT commanded a Market Cap of more than 50,000 Cr

The de-rating has been brutal

Earlier, KPIT was delivering 20%+ constant currency growth & the market kept re-rating the stock aggressively

Yearly CC REV Growth
- 2022: 20.5%
- 2023: 36.7%
- 2024: 39.1%
- 2025: 18.7%
- 2026: 1%

The reason is simple - growth has slowed down significantly & the outlook has also turned weak

At peak, stock was trading at nearly 85x earnings

Now, with growth moderation, multiple has compressed closer to 22x

This is a classic example of what happens when high growth expectations reverse

items.title: RT Equity Insights Elite: For everyone who wants to learn equity research First, understand the basics of accounting & financial statements. After tha...
items.description_html:
RT Equity Insights Elite
For everyone who wants to learn equity research

First, understand the basics of accounting & financial statements.

After that, the best way to learn is by reading companies.

No book/course is required.

Start with one sector.

Read about all the listed companies in that sector. Go through their investor presentations, quarterly results and annual reports.

Once you complete one sector, move to the next sector.

Over time, you will start understanding business models & what actually matters in a business.

The best learning happens when you track companies regularly.

Results & concalls are the most practical way to learn because you get to see how businesses are performing in real time.

And today, if you get stuck anywhere, AI tools can help you understand terms, numbers, business models & industry dynamics.

Everything is freely available.

You just need curiosity, consistency & the habit of reading.

Equity research becomes very interesting once you start connecting the dots across sectors & companies.

items.title: RT Jesús Fernández-Villaverde: This is a photo I took of Keynes’s bedroom at Charleston House, Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant’s farmhouse. Keynes w...
items.description_html:
RT Jesús Fernández-Villaverde
This is a photo I took of Keynes’s bedroom at Charleston House, Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant’s farmhouse. Keynes wrote The Economic Consequences of the Peace at this table, looking out over the pond (also notice Duncan Grant’s 1917 portrait of Keynes in the top left corner, painted in the garden).

Keynes liked the area so much that he later leased Tilton House, just down the lane.

As always, Keynes was a dominating figure. Rumor has it that he changed all the clocks in the house to his own eccentric time zone, “Charleston time,” which suited the household’s bohemian lifestyle. Whether this is true is hard to ascertain, but it fits the atmosphere quite well.

It was most interesting for me to be in the room where a book I teach every fall semester came to life. The curator, however, was intrigued by the fact that I seemed to care far more about Keynes than about Duncan Grant or Vanessa Bell. I apologized to her for my professional bias, pointing out that nobody is perfect!

23 people died in the recent Hauz-Raani fire tragedy in Delhi and a cook was arrested and denied bail for the crime.

Modern cities are a post-colonial reality of India and one that India does not have the wherewithal to deal with. This sits at the heart of the issue and what better way to hide it than to arrest the poor cook as the fall-guy, I argue in this piece for @thewire_in

One downstream consequence of GCC's hiring vs IT

GCC's allocate 110 - 130 sqft per person of office space

Traditional Indian IT companies typically pack workspaces tighter at 80 to 110 sq ft per employee

So for same level of hiring , commercial real estate requirement increases by 10% to 20%

Good for the industry!

items.title: India Accelerates Strategic Disinvestment; LIC Stake Sale Among Key Priorities The Government is fast-tracking stake sales in several major state-owne...
items.description_html:
India Accelerates Strategic Disinvestment; LIC Stake Sale Among Key Priorities

The Government is fast-tracking stake sales in several major state-owned enterprises as part of its broader disinvestment strategy.

The plan includes a potential follow-on stake sale in LIC, India's largest life insurer, along with progress on other strategic divestments.

The objective is to improve public shareholding, raise fiscal resources, and deepen capital markets.

The move follows renewed momentum in the IDBI Bank privatization, indicating a broader push to revive the government's privatization agenda.

items.title: Carlsberg Confidentially Files for India IPO; Targets Up to $700 Million Raise Carlsberg has confidentially filed draft papers for the IPO of its Indi...
items.description_html:
Carlsberg Confidentially Files for India IPO; Targets Up to $700 Million Raise

Carlsberg has confidentially filed draft papers for the IPO of its Indian business.

The offering could raise up to $700 million (₹6,000–6,600 crore) and is expected to be primarily a secondary share sale by the parent company.

Carlsberg India is the country's second-largest brewer, with about 22% market share and 14 breweries.
Kotak Mahindra Capital, JPMorgan and Citi are reported to be advising on the transaction

Government Speeds Up PSU Stake Sales; Invites Fresh Bids from Existing IDBI Bank Suitors

items.title: Nvidia Eyes AI Revenue-Sharing Model, Offering Compute Token Credits to Developers Nvidia is reportedly exploring a new commercial model under which A...
items.description_html:
Nvidia Eyes AI Revenue-Sharing Model, Offering Compute Token Credits to Developers

Nvidia is reportedly exploring a new commercial model under which AI developers receive token-based compute credits to access Nvidia's premium AI hardware.

Instead of paying entirely upfront, developers would share a portion of their future application revenue or token sales with Nvidia.

The initiative aims to reduce the high cost of AI infrastructure for startups while expanding Nvidia's software and inference ecosystem.

The strategy would deepen customer lock-in, encourage more AI application development on Nvidia GPUs, and create a recurring revenue stream beyond one-time hardware sales.

items.title: RT Bull Theory: BREAKING: OpenAI has reportedly proposed giving a 5% equity stake to the U.S. government, according to the Financial Times. At OpenAI'...
items.description_html:
RT Bull Theory
BREAKING: OpenAI has reportedly proposed giving a 5% equity stake to the U.S. government, according to the Financial Times.

At OpenAI's current $852 billion valuation, a 5% stake would be worth $42.6 billion.

CEO Sam Altman has reportedly pitched the idea directly to President Trump, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent as a way to align the public with AI's economic upside and reduce political resistance.

The proposal would encourage other leading U.S. AI companies - including Anthropic, Google and Meta to contribute similar stakes to a sovereign wealth-style fund modeled after the Alaska Permanent Fund, which pays dividends from state investment returns.

The discussions are preliminary and any arrangement would likely require approval from Congress.

items.title: RT Jawwwn: Palantir CEO Alex Karp: "When OpenAI or Anthropic especially are talking about critical infrastructure—it only is being used in our produc...
items.description_html:
RT Jawwwn
Palantir CEO Alex Karp:

"When OpenAI or Anthropic especially are talking about critical infrastructure—it only is being used in our product. My product."

"Just so you see I'm not throwing shade—Dario is a literally historic figure."

"He came from behind, and he's now number one."

"I've been in business for a long time. Never seen anything like this."

"The reality is—critical infrastructure does not run these models without an application layer. In almost all cases, and maybe it won't be all cases someday, that application layer is our ontology. That's a fact."

Via @CNBC


Jawwwn: Palantir CEO Alex Karp on Anthropic's Dario Amodei:

"Most of the things they talk about in public are running on Palantir."

"He's gone from being way behind, to being ahead."

"I'll tell you—he believes what he's saying."

Via @CNBC

items.title: Hero MotoCorp’s bet on Ather Energy may be one of the best examples of a corporate investment in an Indian startup. Hero has already made nearly 8x o...
items.description_html:
Hero MotoCorp’s bet on Ather Energy may be one of the best examples of a corporate investment in an Indian startup.

Hero has already made nearly 8x on its cumulative investment of about Rs 1,700 crore.

Its 30% stake is now worth roughly Rs 13,000 crore.

Ather Energy's market capitalisation touched a new high of Rs 44,000 crore today, nearly four times its valuation at last year's IPO.

Back in 2017, Hero first invested Rs 200 crore for a 28% stake in Ather, then a four-year-old startup valued at just Rs 635 crore at that time.

Hero continued backing the company through multiple funding rounds and the IPO, and is now a co-promoter alongside founders Tarun Mehta and Swapnil Jain - who together own about a 10% stake.

items.description_html:
Oyo is required to "use all net cash proceeds" - including through the IPO - towards the prepayment of the term loan as a part of its credit agreement.

Oyo has already paid out close to Rs 1,100 crore in debt servicing in nine months of FY26 - a number which keeps rising.


Madhav Chanchani: Oyo plans to use Rs 4,988 crore from the proposed Rs 6,650 crore IPO - about 75% - to repay its massive debt.

It still has term loans (TLB) of over Rs 7,000 crore.

Most of the debt dates back to $825 mn TLB in 2021, but remaining to its Motel 6 acquisition two years ago.

India now accounts for less than 12% of gross bookings, down from 30% a couple of years ago.

This is Oyo's third filing for a public listing, after attempts at going public in 2021 and 2023.

Besides founder Ritesh Agarwal, Softbank - which owns 40% stake - also listed as promoter.

Shadowfax is also growing much faster (69% vs 8%) despite Blue Dart generating higher revenue (Rs 6,150 crore vs Rs 4,200 crore).

Meanwhile, Delhivery's revenue crossed Rs 10,000 crore in FY26.


Madhav Chanchani: Delhivery's Sahil Barua says that he doesn't see a place in the market for a fourth independent logistics player beyond the three listed players.

He is taking a shot at XpressBees, backed by Blackstone.

"There's still room for one or two players to exit the market gracefully."
